description: The .bep file hosts two distinct data categories: versioned plan data and immutable curated artifacts.
High-write-frequency or randomly-accessed data lives outside the .bep for operational use; it may still be included as an immutable archival snapshot.
baseline/ must always be a full structural mirror of the .bep root for any collection with a lifecycle distinct from the plan.
Each operation (commit, discard, reset, squash) does exactly what its target specifies, with no implicit side effects on other targets.
Version history is stored as inverse diffs applied backward from the current state, so bep.json always reflects the latest version at zero reconstruction cost.
BEPs are versioned as two numbers, {major}.{minor} — minor for in-progress patches, major for significant milestones.
Relationships between schema entities are expressed via ID references, never by nesting the referenced entity inline.
Schema keys are always fully spelled out, never abbreviated, so they are legible without external documentation.
Data that can be derived deterministically from existing schema entities is computed on demand, never persisted as its own field or entity.
The format includes constructs designed for direct LLM authorship and consumption (skills, memories, flags), each using the representation natural to that purpose.
Memory IDs are human-readable kebab-case slugs, not UUIDs, so references are legible directly in markdown and filenames.
